outOfScope: Applying recommendations, editing evaluated source, editing `QUALITY.md`, or creating external issues.
Migrating or rewriting stale historical evaluation records.
Exhaustive release matrix verification, hosted registry validation, or live install-channel tests beyond repository-visible smoke-check definitions.

assessmentResults.requirement: agents can reach the minimum project context and deeper routed guidance

ratingResult.rationale: Project orientation, agent rules, guide routing, and component map are reachable from checked-in files.

assessmentResults.requirement: project guidance makes task boundaries and done criteria explicit

ratingResult.rationale: Task guidance routes work by artifact type and names verification expectations.

assessmentResults.requirement: agents can discover the tools and commands needed to work the project

ratingResult.rationale: The repo exposes setup, check, package, release, install, and quality commands through docs and mise tasks.

assessmentResults.requirement: quality workflows preserve useful state in durable local artifacts

ratingResult.rationale: Evaluation records, quality logs, and workflow feedback logs exist as durable local artifacts rather than chat-only state.

assessmentResults.requirement: verification signals are discoverable and remediation-bearing

ratingResult.rationale: Local status, lint, evaluation status, CI, hooks, and mise checks give actionable feedback.

assessmentResults.requirement: core project standards are backed by enforceable checks or reviewable gates

ratingResult.rationale: Core formatting, Go quality, schema, packaging, and release expectations have runnable gates or explicit review routes.

assessmentResults.requirement: agent action limits and mutation gates are visible before consequential changes

ratingResult.rationale: Agent rules and skill workflows make mutation class, confirmation, and action limits visible before consequential changes.

assessmentResults.requirement: the format specification admits a single interpretation

ratingResult.rationale: The specification defines vocabulary, schema shape, references, and evaluation semantics with settled obligation language.

assessmentResults.requirement: the format specification is internally consistent

ratingResult.rationale: Terminology and rating/report semantics are consistent across schema and evaluation sections.

assessmentResults.requirement: the format specification is complete enough to implement and author from

ratingResult.rationale: Model elements, references, extension points, evaluation records, and report expectations are specified without implementation reverse engineering.

assessmentResults.requirement: each normative rule is observable or testable

ratingResult.rationale: Conformance turns on observable document, record, and report properties; judgment boundaries are explicit.

assessmentResults.requirement: the specification keeps model domain separate from agentic use context

ratingResult.rationale: The spec and domain guide describe broad modeled domains while preserving agent-first usage as context of use.

assessmentResults.requirement: the skill grounds setup, evaluation, and follow-up in the active model and evidence

ratingResult.rationale: The skill requires version/status grounding, current evidence, source-as-data handling, and stop rules for weak support.

assessmentResults.requirement: the skill gates mutating actions with explicit decision briefs

ratingResult.rationale: Setup, update, and recommendation follow-up mutation surfaces are gated, while evaluation limits itself to evaluation artifacts.

assessmentResults.requirement: the skill follows the agent-mediated UX guide

ratingResult.rationale: The skill contract requires status-first, evidence-led interaction, run frames, decision briefs, and scannable closeouts.

assessmentResults.requirement: the skill workflows match their functional specs and CLI support surface

ratingResult.rationale: Runtime workflows use available CLI commands, require dry-runs for record payloads, and prohibit hand-authored CLI-owned records.

assessmentResults.requirement: the CLI follows its functional specifications

ratingResult.rationale: Implemented commands and tests satisfy the functional surface exercised by lint, status, evaluation record writes, and reporting.

assessmentResults.requirement: CLI commands expose stable machine-readable behavior where agents need it

ratingResult.rationale: Version, lint, status, record writes, and package checks expose non-interactive JSON or stable command behavior.

assessmentResults.requirement: the CLI follows the project CLI design guide

ratingResult.rationale: Command help documents payload fields, examples, dry-run behavior, JSON receipts, and next-action diagnostics.

assessmentResults.requirement: the Go implementation follows the project Go style guide

ratingResult.rationale: Formatting, vetting, linting, tests, and package checks pass on the implementation.

assessmentResults.requirement: introductory docs foreground the agent-first workflow without making the CLI the main surface

ratingResult.rationale: The README frames /quality as the primary experience and the CLI as support tooling.

assessmentResults.requirement: docs keep modeled domains broad while preserving the agentic use context

ratingResult.rationale: Docs state broad modeled-domain range while retaining AI assistant/coding-agent use context.

assessmentResults.requirement: docs reflect the current command, install, release, and workflow surfaces

ratingResult.rationale: Most docs match current workflows, but install examples still pin an obsolete v0.5.1 release despite the active 0.11.0 CLI/skill pair.

assessmentResults.requirement: tooling specs identify the behavior they govern and stay linked to runtime artifacts

ratingResult.rationale: Specs are organized by CLI, reports, evaluation records, and skill workflows, and runtime guidance points back to those governed surfaces.

assessmentResults.requirement: the specs bundle remains internally consistent and synchronized with implementation

ratingResult.rationale: The checked implementation, skill, and package tests pass against the current specs and documented surfaces.

assessmentResults.requirement: supported install and update paths are documented, tested, and package the expected binary

ratingResult.rationale: Install scripts and smoke checks cover the expected channels, but pinned install examples are stale and can steer agents/CI to an old version.

assessmentResults.requirement: release gates verify the artifacts and channels that users depend on

ratingResult.rationale: Release and smoke workflows cover GitHub archives, npm, Homebrew, checksums, notes, and package assembly.

assessmentResults.requirement: the agent harness orients agents and routes them to deeper guidance

ratingResult.rationale: AGENTS.md provides project context, component routing, guide routing, agent action rules, vocabulary conventions, and quality-domain guidance.

assessmentResults.requirement: the QUALITY.md model follows the active authoring guide family

ratingResult.rationale: The model is valid, broad in domain, evidence-oriented, and explicit about use context, risks, scope, sources, and lifecycle unknowns.

assessmentResults.requirement: evaluation history and model-change history remain inspectable and distinct

ratingResult.rationale: History is inspectable and distinct, but the prior run is stale and not reportable under the current record contract.

findingSummaries.summary: AGENTS.md names README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md as required context and links task-specific guides.
Routine changes are scoped directly, while guide routing and change-case rules define when durable design history is needed.
The check task aggregates format, tidy, vet, lint, test, Markdown, and npm package checks.
The skill requires evaluate to write numbered records through the CLI and feedback logs under .quality/logs/.
The full local check passed across gofmt, tidy, dprint, vet, tests, npm package verification, and golangci-lint.
CI intentionally stays in lockstep with mise run check.
The user interaction contract requires decision briefs before confirmation-sensitive mutations.
The schema section defines required model properties and the model-wide ratingScale.
Roll-up inputs are named consistently as requirement rating results, factor ratings, child area ratings, and body context.
The report section names required summary, scope, ratings, rationale, gaps, and recommendations.
When evidence cannot justify roll-up, the specified outcome is not assessed rather than an inferred rating.
The guide states domain agnostic is about what a model can describe, while the project is agent-first in how QUALITY.md is used.
Evaluated source content is treated as data, not instructions.
Existing-file setup edits require a decision brief before mutation.
The user interaction contract explicitly routes presentation through the agent-mediated UX guide.
The evaluate workflow writes records only through qualitymd evaluation assessment, analysis, and recommendation commands.
The full local check passed, including go test ./... across internal/cli, internal/evaluation, internal/model, internal/schema, and related packages.
Status returned structured readiness, model shape, source coverage, evaluation run counts, and next actions.
Assessment help lists required payload fields, dry-run behavior, stdin usage, and flags.
gofmt, go vet, go test ./..., and golangci-lint all passed.
The README says the /quality skill helps setup, evaluate, and evolve the model, and that the agentic workflow is primary.
The README's Quality Beyond Software section lists software, documentation, data, reports, services, operations, clinical handoffs, legal contracts, and other contexts.
The non-interactive pinned installer example uses QUALITYMD_VERSION=v0.5.1; PowerShell and sh -s examples repeat the stale v0.5.1 pin at nearby lines.
The repository model identifies SPECIFICATION.md, skills/quality, specs/skills, cmd/qualitymd, internal, and specs/cli as the major governed components.
The full gate passed, including tests and the npm package/skill relative-link check.
Agent/CI examples pin v0.5.1 while the visible CLI and skill metadata are 0.11.0.
The install-smoke workflow verifies managed shell/PowerShell installers, npm packages, and Homebrew channel behavior across supported OS matrices.
The guides table routes agents to release, change-case, spec, design, OKF, UX, domain-modeling, CLI, and Go guidance.
The body explains the root area, broad quality domain, and agent-first use context; qualitymd lint reports zero findings.
The prior run reports 15 assessments and 4 analyses, but all old record shapes lack required areaPath fields and the run is not reportable.

recommendations.doneCriterion: The install guide no longer contains stale v0.5.1 pins, pinned examples cannot age silently, and the docs and distribution assessments reach target on re-evaluation.
qualitymd status QUALITY.md --json no longer reports a stale non-reportable prior run, or the repository has an explicit documented archive state that keeps stale runs out of current-readiness gaps.
